title: Earnings: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Campus reported net income of $1,712K as of December 31, 2026. Interest income totaled $10,792K, while interest expense amounted to $2,989K, resulting in a spread that supported profitability despite significant year-over-year declines across the board.

Non-interest income totaled $2,608K and remains a modest contributor relative to net income. The majority of this income is generated from other non-interest income ($1380), with additional contributions from fee income ($19).

Non-interest expense totaled $8,359K, reflecting the ongoing costs required to support daily operations and member services. The largest component is credit loss expense ($340), followed by employee compensation expenses (implied within total non-interest expense structure) and other operational line items.

Overall Position:
Campus continues to generate softer earnings supported by interest income as its dominant driver. Non-interest income plays a limited role in overall revenue, while expense levels remain necessary for operations. Sustaining performance will depend on managing credit losses and maintaining stable fee-based streams.
